title: Prime Minister Worries About Kanye West at Music Festival
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is worried because Kanye West, a famous rapper, is going to perform at a big music festival in London this summer. Kanye West has said mean things about Jewish people in the past, which is called antisemitism, and he even said nice things about Nazis, who were very bad people in history. The Prime Minister thinks this is not okay and wants everyone to make sure Jewish people feel safe in Britain. The festival's main sponsor, Pepsi, has decided to stop supporting the event because of this. Some other leaders think Kanye West should not be allowed to come to the UK. Kanye West has apologized for his comments before, but people are still concerned. The festival is called Wireless and happens in north London for three days.

keywords.explanation: a big event with music and fun
being mean or unfair to Jewish people
a company that gives money to support an event
saying sorry for something you did wrong

options: He moved to a new city

background_read: This article is about a music festival in London where Kanye West, a popular rapper, is scheduled to perform. Some people, including the Prime Minister, are worried because Kanye has made antisemitic comments in the past, which are hurtful to Jewish people. Antisemitism is a form of prejudice, and it's important to stand against it to keep everyone safe and respected. Festivals like Wireless are fun events, but they also need to think about who they invite to make sure everyone feels welcome. Sponsors like Pepsi help pay for festivals, and they can choose to stop if they don't agree with the performers.
Article_Structure: WHO: Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Kanye West
WHAT: Concerns about Kanye West performing at Wireless Festival due to past antisemitic remarks
WHERE: London, at the Wireless Festival
WHY: To ensure safety and respect for Jewish people and address antisemitism

perspectives.description: The Prime Minister and others worry that inviting Kanye West might make Jewish people feel unsafe and promote antisemitism.
